bibliography: Computer Organization and Design, The Hardware Software Interface, RISC-V Edition, Second Edition. David Patterson & John Hennessy. Elsevier, 2021.
methodology: The use of modern and up-to-date simulators allows for detailed visualization and learning of how the processor and memory work.
objectives: Understanding how computers work, focusing on the interaction between programs and hardware. Recognizing the potential and limitations of a computer. Understanding how the various elements of a computer's structure/architecture interact.
organicUnit: ECATI - School of Communication, Architecture, Arts and Information Technologies
programme:
Program


	Introduction to Computer Architecture
	Machine Instructions
	Processor
	Memory
	Parallelism


In the Practical Classes, students do exercises and use simulators to run programs and observe their interaction with the hardware.
presentation: This course describes the workings of a modern computer focusing on the interface between programs and the hardware. Students learn about the components of a computer, machine instructions, how a processor operates, how memory is orgsanized and how it functionn, and parallelism as a way to improve performance. The study is based on the 32-bit RISC-V processor.
avaliacao:
50% avaliação teórica

  • Frequência intercalar. Peso: 50% teórica
  • Frequência final: Peso: 50% teórica
  • Exame recurso: Peso: 100% teórica
  • As frequências e exame são feitos sem consulta (livros, apontamentos, IA, etc.)

50% avaliação prática

  • 8 fichas feitas nas aulas práticas, 40% da nota prática, notas 0/100%
  • 1 trabalho prático, 60% da nota prática, nota mínima 9,5
  • Fichas e trabalhos feitos individualmente ou por grupos de 2 estudantes

Notas mínimas:

  • Componente teórica: 9,5 valores (média da frequência intercalar e da frequência final, ou do exame de recurso)
  • Componente prática: 9,5 valores no trabalho e média de 9,5 valores no conjunto de trabalho e fichas, ou 9,5 valores no trabalho de recurso
